description: A horizontal full-color candid photograph taken inside an industrial training/workshop facility (visible overhead gantry crane, blue lockers/cabinets, fluorescent lighting). At center-left a woman with long dark hair, wearing a bright green blazer and a white hard hat bearing a circular agency seal and safety glasses, is shaking hands with a young man on the right; she appears to be U.S. Secretary of Labor Lori Chavez-DeRemer. The young man she greets wears a dark green t-shirt, a yellow hard hat marked '740', safety glasses, and a beard. To his left stands another young man in a black t-shirt with a black hard hat labeled 'JOSHUA COLE' and safety glasses, plus a forearm tattoo. Other men in hard hats, polos and one in what appears to be a military/Navy uniform stand in the background; a man in a blue polo at far left and men in white shirts/ties observe. One gray hard hat at far left reads '900T'. There is no on-image graphic text, headline, slogan or agency logo overlay; the agency seal appears only physically on the Secretary's hard hat. This is a routine event/grip-and-grin photograph documenting a site visit, not a designed graphic. The accompanying tweet frames it for National Tradesmen Day honoring skilled workers as 'the backbone of our nation,' but the image itself carries no designed messaging overlay.
notable_text: (no on-image graphic text; hard hats read '900T', '740', 'JOSHUA COLE')
